artifacts.title: Which receipt from 2022-10-17
artifacts.excerpt: The more that other tools push past @RoamResearch the more I come to realize that the compromises Roam made weren't merely shortcuts/workarounds, but actually elegant solutions to really hard problems; ones for which *any* solutions are hard to come by, given the tradeoffs.

artifacts.title: Which receipt from 2021-02-02
artifacts.excerpt: One of the topics I touched on with @RoamFm last night was 'service', and when I think about 'service' I think about the selflessness with which so many members of #roamcult show up. I'm fortunate enough to bear witness to some of the behind-the-scenes work and it's breathtaking.
artifacts.proof_of_search: Community Archive row → normalized post → topic planet. Terms: which, feels, experience, think.

artifacts.title: Which receipt from 2021-09-24

artifacts.title: Think receipt from 2022-12-13
artifacts.excerpt:
@utotranslucence I think the leverage points are
- make fewer, higher quality commitments
- diversify points-of-contact with each commitment (if it only lives in one chat thread, app, or watering hole, it will die there)
- set flakiness baseline expectations explicitly (underpromise, overdeliver)
artifacts.proof_of_search: Community Archive row → normalized post → topic planet. Terms: think, don, something, work.

artifacts.title: Think receipt from 2024-01-09
artifacts.excerpt:
@eigenrobot i think for our age group in particular, youth is conflated with finite and rate-limited media, so if you didn’t pace yourself or stock up properly you experienced lack of access

today boredom accompanies ubiquitous infinite media access so its phenomenology is different
artifacts.proof_of_search: Community Archive row → normalized post → topic planet. Terms: think, don, something, work.
